title: rosa × pervirens var. typica
intro: Rosa × pervirens var. typica, also known as the wild rose or Scots rose, is a deciduous shrub native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Rosa × pervirens var. typica include soothing digestive problems, reducing stress and anxiety, and promoting skin health due to its high content of vitamins and flavonoids. Herbal preparations of this plant are often used in teas, tinctures, and topical creams, taking advantage of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties to promote overall well-being.
